module_signals.finding: Reported business facts are high-confidence (audited, fast-scaling AI cloud, two blue-chip anchor contracts, ~net-cash at 9/30/25). Forward economics are low-confidence (deal margins, financing/dilution path, capacity-to-revenue conversion all undisclosed). Evidence is adequate to act at a tested size; the unknowns become a confidence haircut on size, not a WATCH.

Real demand-exceeds-supply bottleneck: the AI build-out's binding constraint moved from chips to energized power + ready data-center capacity; NBIS is a pure-play renting that scarce capacity to creditworthy payers. 'Sold out of capacity'; two anchor contracts (Microsoft ~$17.4-19.4B/2031, Meta ~$3B/5yr); IEA corroborates power as the structural bottleneck. Forcing metric = connected MW + realized ARR vs the $7-9B CYE2026 target. exceptional_bottleneck.

NBIS sits on the rent-earning node (energized, networked, contract-ready capacity), vertically integrated with its own software stack, NVIDIA as supplier+shareholder, and a rare ex-Yandex team that built this category before (operator grade 4/5 lifts the execution leg). Capped below +2 by undisclosed contract spread, customer concentration (cf. CoreWeave 62% MSFT), deep-pocketed competition, and a governance/key-man durability discount (counted once).

Operating trend confirms (+1 on its own): rev +39% QoQ to $146.1M, group adj-EBITDA loss collapsed -$266M FY24 -> -$5.2M Q3'25, core adj-EBITDA positive ~19%, conservative 4yr depreciation, candid QoE (Q2 GAAP profit flagged as a Toloka revaluation, not operations). Funding reality warns (-1 on its own): 9M op cash -$432.4M, 9M PP&E -$2,010.0M, accelerating capex, explicit equity+ATM dependence, unproven asset-backed debt, delivery-contingent backlog. Net 0 -> size cap + financing kill-gate, not a veto (b/s net-cash at 9/30/25, no near maturities).

Dominant kill path is reflexive financing dependence: a multi-tens-of-billions build pre-funded with external capital is reflexive - a growth/capex-cycle wobble makes the ATM/raises dilutive or unavailable, slows the build, misses ARR, compresses the multiple, worsening the next raise (permanent per-share impairment without bankruptcy). Plus customer concentration (Microsoft+Meta, mix undisclosed), unproven build spread, capacity-conversion slip (CoreWeave precedent), and governance/key-man/geopolitical. Serious, survivable, value-impairment risks -> risk penalty + hard kill-gates, NO hard veto. Not a structural_decline or false_cheap trap.

At $83.71 (~$21.1B cap) the stock is BELOW both the Sept-2025 equity print ($92.50) and the convertible conversion reference ($138.75) and into a soft, contested tape (15.5% short interest, no index bid) - a structured entry, not a chase. Optically extreme on trailing revenue, but for an exceptional_bottleneck the trap is under-sizing the starter on optics. Survivable downside at small size supports a risk-controlled STARTER scaling to a Confirmed (not Core) ceiling on delivery.

starter_zone: <= ~$92.50 (at/under the Sept-2025 equity print); current $83.71 qualifies
add_zone: add on CONFIRMATION, not lower price alone: connected-MW tracking the 220MW CYE2025 / 800MW-1GW CYE2026 guide AND realized ARR on the $7-9B path AND a non-punitive financing (esp. asset-backed debt on attractive terms). Prefer adding into proof; opportunistic adds <= ~$70 only if thesis intact.
trim_or_no_chase_zone: no-chase well above the ~$138.75 convertible reference absent a step-up in proven economics; trim only on a broken thesis (see kill criteria), not on weight alone
binding_constraint: Reflexive financing dependence: funding the >=2.5GW build needs continuous external capital (equity/ATM + still-unproven asset-backed debt). The thesis stands or falls on financing the build cheaply (without crushing per-share value) AND converting contracted power into connected, revenue-producing capacity. Customer concentration (Microsoft+Meta) and the undisclosed contract spread are the co-binding economic unknowns.
kill_criteria: Connected power materially lags the 220MW CYE2025 / 800MW-1GW CYE2026 guide (delivery/conversion slip).
A heavily dilutive financing event (large equity/ATM issuance well below intrinsic) OR asset-backed debt fails to materialize on attractive terms -> funding model broken.
Disclosure or strong evidence that anchor-contract margins are thin / the build earns a sub-cost-of-capital return (ARR-vanity trap).
Customer-concentration shock: Microsoft or Meta pauses, renegotiates down, or in-sources.
Realized ARR badly undershoots the trajectory toward the $7-9B CYE2026 target (ramp stalls).
Founder/control risk crystallizes: Volozh exit without a credible successor, loss of a core technologist (Korolenko/Shtan), CFO churn < ~18 months, or value-destroying use of dual-class control.
runner_dissent: Two-sided. (1) The exceptional_bottleneck size discipline pushes toward a clear STARTER and warns against under-sizing on optics - I am comfortable that no hard veto fires (survivable, net-cash, real blue-chip backlog, A-team). (2) But this is the textbook reflexive financing setup, the as-of is RECENT, and the most thesis-deciding facts (contract margins, the dilution path, capacity conversion) are exactly the undisclosed ones - so I deliberately set the initial at the low end of Starter (~3%) and cap max at the Confirmed tier (~10%), NOT Core, until financing-without-dilution and connected-MW/ARR are proven. Note the one-factor portfolio caution (METHODOLOGY 5): NBIS + BTC + GOOGL all ride 'AI + liquidity' - a real hidden single bet that argues for the smaller end of the size band. Also carrying the Q3-letter $4.3B-vs-$7-9B ARR cover discrepancy as model risk (resolved only with as-of data, not hindsight).
